description: Compliance-shaped finding asserts a provincial amendment shortens the fire-pump acceptance-testing interval; verification finds the cited requirement is unamended base national-code / NFPA 25 text, so the jurisdiction attribution is corrected. One of the field trial's three refutation wins (D-4 [FT]).

finding.issue: The package should cite the provincial fire-code amendment that is claimed to shorten the fire-pump acceptance-testing interval below the base standard (profile requirement r-a1b2c3d4e5f6).
finding.existingText: Test fire pumps annually per NFPA 25.
finding.replacementText: Test fire pumps at the interval required by the provincial fire-code amendment.

paragraph_map_slice.text: A. Test fire pumps annually per NFPA 25.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: The cited fire-pump acceptance-testing interval is the base national-code / NFPA 25 requirement, not a provincial amendment. The province adopts the referenced standard without shortening the interval, so attributing the requirement to a local amendment misstates its source; the base-standard interval governs and no amendment edit is warranted.
captured_verifier_response.correction: the base standard interval governs — no provincial amendment shortens it

ground_truth.correct_correction_text: the base standard interval governs — no provincial amendment shortens it
ground_truth.notes: Jurisdiction-attribution error: base national-code text presented as a provincial amendment. Claim-level verification catches the misattribution (D-4 [FT]; §9 Q2). Compliance findings always ride round-2 verification precisely so this class is caught before publication.
